reasoning: The Nov 7 baseline 1-year yield of 3.49% reflects a local short-term dip from 3.56%, making a mean-reverting bounce by Nov 16 highly likely. By Dec 9 and Feb 7, although the Fed is expected to cut rates, the market has already aggressively priced in the December policy easing (indicated by the current yield being well below the fed funds target of 3.75-4.00%). Sticky inflation and concerns surrounding expansionist fiscal policy likely mean yields will correct upward rather than fall below 3.49%, though over a longer horizon macro easing balances this. Adhering to the rule that distant dates must be closer to 0.5, probabilities taper toward 0.50 on later dates.
